title: turraea ovata
intro: Turraea ovata, also known as the West African Yellow Wood, is a plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of turraenin, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced digestion. Turraea ovata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, making it a versatile and accessible natural remedy.
